title: crataegus laevigata subsp. microphylla
intro: Crataegus laevigata subsp. microphylla, also known as the small-leaved hawthorn, is a subspecies of the Crataegus laevigata plant, which is a type of flowering shrub or small tree belonging to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and act as a cardiac tonic. The health benefits of Crataegus laevigata subsp. microphylla include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to promote heart health and well-being.
